claim: A Conflict in DECIDED(...) lifecycle shall carry a decided_by: Stakeholder.id field (later: a cryptographic signature) — enforced by a new invariant.
why: SETTLED (P3): Conflict.decided_by field added; check_decided_has_decided_by fires when lifecycle starts with DECIDED but decided_by is empty or owned by a member. Makes the hard boundary structural at the decision moment — the AI cannot silently write DECIDED without naming a human decider who is outside the conflict's members.

claim: An Operator shall be a frozen dataclass in tensio.operator with typed anchor 'OP-'.
why: Atom of R-operator-acting-facet (type identity concern). tensio.operator.Operator is a frozen dataclass; OP-director is the first instance.

claim: An Operator.stakeholder shall reference an existing Stakeholder.id.
why: Atom of R-operator-acting-facet (stakeholder reference concern). check_no_dangling_ids validates the reference.

claim: Goal shall be its own frozen dataclass type (not a Requirement facet) with typed anchor 'GOAL-'.
why: Atom of R-goal-as-target-state (type identity concern). tensio/process.py defines Goal as a frozen dataclass with GOAL_LIFECYCLE. M19 resolved.

claim: Goal.owner shall reference an existing Operator.id.
why: Atom of R-goal-as-target-state (ownership concern). check_goal_owner_is_operator and check_no_dangling_ids validate.

claim: Every Conflict node shall carry a non-empty axis, context, and steward.
why: These three fields are the knowledge that makes the contradiction visible (R-conflict-is-connector-node). A Conflict missing any of them is an invisible contradiction. Atomized claim — one structural rule with three required fields, all enforced by the same check_*.

claim: Every Requirement.m_tag (when non-empty) shall match `^M[1-9][0-9]*$`, be unique across the graph, and appear only on OPEN requirements.
why: The M-decision registry (DECISIONS.md) bijection depends on m_tag discipline. Was orphan policy; now claimed. Atomic — one concern (M-tag well-formedness) with three sub-rules all in one check_*; if we later split the check, this R splits with it.

claim: Requirement.status shall validate against the framework-supplied REQUIREMENT_STATUS_LIFECYCLE.
why: Atom of R-lifecycle-abstraction (requirement validation concern). check_status_in_lifecycle validates Requirement statuses on every invariant run.

claim: Conflict.lifecycle shall validate against the framework-supplied CONFLICT_LIFECYCLE.
why: Atom of R-lifecycle-abstraction (conflict validation concern). check_status_in_lifecycle validates Conflict lifecycles on every invariant run.

claim: check_typed_anchors shall validate PR- and GOAL- prefixes for Process and Goal nodes.
why: Atom of R-process-aspect-first (typed-anchors concern). Without prefix validation, Process and Goal nodes escape the anchoring discipline.

claim: Every `domains/<name>/` directory contains `manifest.py` defining top-level constants ID, DESCRIPTION, GOALS, DIRECTOR.
why: The manifest is the machine-readable identity of the domain: it lets create_domain, gen_spec, and the root DOMAIN-MAP block discover a domain's metadata without loading its full graph. GOALS and DIRECTOR are required so any operator can reconstitute context from the manifest alone (R-agent-never-lost). ENFORCED via check_domain_manifest_valid (task #64).
